title: Star Wars: Union
content:
{{Top|rwm|new|italics=1}} {{ComicSeries |image=[[File:Union_art.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Union'' |writer=[[Michael A. Stackpole]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |penciller=[[Robert Teranishi]] |inker=[[Christopher Chuckry]] |letterer=[[Vickie Williams]] |colorist=[[Christopher Chuckry]] |cover artist=[[Duncan Fegredo]] |editor=[[Peet Janes]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |start date=[[November 10]], [[1999]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/98-433/Star-Wars-Union-1-of-4|text=Star Wars: Union 1}} |end date=[[February 16]], [[2000]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/98-447/Star-Wars-Union-4-of-4|text=Star Wars: Union 4}} |schedule=Monthly |format=Completed |issues=4 |timeline=[[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] {{DarkHorse|url=Zones/StarWars/Timeline|text=title=Dark Horse Comics timeline}} |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} {{Quote|It's all so beautiful. Like an illusion...a dream. Too beautiful...has to be a nightmare.|Mara Jade Skywalker|''[[Star Wars: Union]]''}} '''''Star Wars: Union''''' is a four-issue [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]], published between [[November]] [[1999]] and [[February]] [[2000]]. The series' plot focused on the [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|marriage]] of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''When Luke Skywalker, the New Republic's greatest hero and sole Jedi Master, decides to marry Mara Jade, the woman who was once the Emperor's personal assassin, you can be sure that hands both Imperial and New Republican will be raised to stop the marriage—at any cost! Written by acclaimed ''Star Wars'' novelist Michael Stackpole (''X-Wing: Rogue Squadron''; ''I, Jedi'') and illustrated with photographic realism by Robert Teranishi, ''Union'' spotlights a monumental event in the ''Star Wars'' timeline that bridges the classic ''Star Wars'' trilogy to its boundless future a long time ago in a galaxy far, far away!'' ==Plot summary== {{Plot}} ==Continuity== Though ''[[Vision of the Future]]'' ends with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara]] intending to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]], their actual wedding would not be depicted in a novel. Instead, writer [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and artist [[Robert Teranishi]] did the honors with [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[1999]] – [[2000]]. The four part miniseries ''Star Wars Union'' is set [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], right after the events of the [[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology|''Hand of Thrawn Duology'']]. The [[Star Wars: Young Jedi Knights|''Young Jedi Knight'']] novels, published years before ''Hand of Thrawn'' and ''Union'', were set after the wedding, but no mention was made of Luke and Mara being married. In fact, Mara was conspicuously absent from those books set in [[23 ABY/Legends|23]]-[[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]. This was a deliberate omission by the authors of those works, who knew Luke would be married at this point in the chronology, but could not make mention of it until after ''Hand of Thrawn'' was published. A banner in the exhibition on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] says "[[Death Star/Legends|DEATH STAR]]" in [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] letters. Also, the "IDs" of [[Banner Sumptor]], [[Chala Venan]], [[Chik Apla]], [[Iry Danta]], [[Anlys Takkar]], and [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Derran Takkar]] show their names written in both Aurebesh and [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]]. ''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'', mistakenly places this tale in [[20 ABY/Legends|20 ABY]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Union}} ===Issues=== {| {{Prettytable}} ! colspan="3" |Color code key: |- |style="background-color:#FFBB60;" align="center" width="33%" |Collected (TPB) issue |style="background-color:#40CC40;" align="center" width="33%" |Released issue |style="background-color:#FFFF80;" align="center" width="33%" |Future issue |} {| {{Prettytable}} !
